_comment: Data-driven intent classification rules for DE/BZF phraseology. Loaded by src/atc/intent_rules.cpp. Schema: see intent_rules.hpp. DE-Region-specific rules: additive to EN keywords so BZF transcripts with mixed EN/DE words ('Friedrichshafen Tower' / 'in sight') still classify. Note: intent_parser::parse() runs de_phraseology::parse_spoken_number() before this layer, so ziffernweise BZF pronunciation ('eins null eins drei') already arrives as raw digits ('1013') -- rules below match on numeric form.

rules._note: Profanity / insults toward ATC. Highest priority so tone is caught even when other intent keywords are present.
Pilot-issued correction phraseology. 'negativ' alone is BZF's 'no'; 'negativer kontakt' is a traffic-advisory reply and must NOT be claimed here.
NfL ANLAGE 7.4 a) RMZ-Einflug/-Durchflug. 'rmz'-Anker macht es kollisionsfrei zu INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND (verlangt has_facility turm).
NfL ANLAGE 7.4 b) RMZ-Verlassen. Kein 'frequenz'/'wiederhoeren' -> kollisionsfrei zu LEAVING_FREQUENCY.
Traffic-advisory replies must come BEFORE SELF_ANNOUNCE.

Readback. DE phrases: 'verstanden', 'rollen Sie' (ATC instruction echo), 'Rollhalt'/'Haltepunkt', 'kontaktiere', 'Startfreigabe'/'Landefreigabe'. Also the compact Tower echo phrases 'Start frei' (take-off clearance readback) and 'Landung frei' (landing clearance readback) — Whisper renders the Tower's wording verbatim, so the pilot's repeat-back shows up as the same short tokens. A post-landing adjustment below demotes 'Landung frei' to RUNWAY_VACATED when at_airport_after_landing fires. The position/departure echo patterns require the Sie-imperative 'melden sie' (the Tower instruction being read back) — NOT the bare verb 'melde': a pilot's first-person report 'melde Endanflug/Gegenanflug/abflugbereit' is a REPORT_POSITION_*/READY_FOR_DEPARTURE act, not a readback, and must fall through to those rules.
VFR cross-country departure: ready-for-departure plus a course/direction marker.
Parking/apron/etc. as the destination -- NOT pilot's location.

Runway-vacated. DE: 'Piste frei' (free), 'Piste verlassen' (left), 'Piste geraeumt' (cleared). Pilots in real BZF traffic say 'Piste 06 frei' with the runway number in between, so we also accept 'piste' + 'frei' as separate tokens. 'landung'/'start' guards keep the Tower-echo readbacks ('Landung frei', 'Start frei') out — those are handled by READBACK above.
Plain 'request taxi' / 'rollen'. Exclude clearance readbacks ('rollen sie ueber X') and frequency-change readbacks that mention 'taxi'.

Position-Reports. 'melden sie' (Tower-Imperativ) und 'report' (EN-Imperativ) ausschliessen, damit der Pilot-Readback der Anweisung 'melden Sie Gegenanflug/Endanflug' nicht falsch als Position-Report klassifiziert wird. Echo der Tower-Anweisung gehoert zu READBACK. 'gegneranflug' und 'gegen anflug' fangen haeufige Whisper-Fehltranskriptionen von 'Gegenanflug' ab.
Inbound/landing request, but only when no facility keyword (Tower/Turm) is present.
Tower call with inbound/landing intent -- beats INITIAL_CALL_TOWER below.
Last-resort fallback for traffic-advisory replies. Single keyword 'looking' / 'halte ausschau' is ambiguous; confidence is low.

adjustments._note: Upgrade INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND -> INITIAL_CALL_INBOUND_VRP when a VRP was named.
Airborne intent while on ground -- likely a readback. Demote to READBACK.
Ground intent while airborne -- physically impossible.
SELF_ANNOUNCE only makes sense on UNICOM at non-towered fields.
Pilot called 'tower' at a non-towered airport -- phraseology error.
DE counterpart: pilot called 'turm' at a non-towered airport.

Post-landing 'request taxi' / 'rollen' is a taxi-to-apron request as long as the pilot is still in the post-landing state window (on the ground, last landing in history, no new DEPARTURE_CLEARED yet). State window instead of the 120 s just_landed clock so a long roll-out plus slow taxi-back still hits the demotion. Mirrors the at_airport_after_landing choice in the 'Landung frei' demote rule below.

Post-landing taxi-clearance read-back. The Tower's 'rollen Sie zum GAT-Vorfeld ueber Alpha' is echoed by the pilot as 'Rolle GAT-Vorfeld ueber Alpha' / 'Rolle zu GA Parking via Alpha'. That first-person phrasing misses both the READBACK rule ('rollen sie' Tower imperative) and the REQUEST_TAXI_PARKING rule ('rollen zum vorfeld'), so it lands as UNKNOWN and used to bounce off the LM as _INVALID. While in the post-landing window, an UNKNOWN utterance that names the apron/parking destination is the clearance read-back -- accept it silently as READBACK. A genuine fresh taxi-parking request classifies as REQUEST_TAXI_PARKING (not UNKNOWN), so it is untouched. One entry per destination token because text_contains is a single substring.

Post-landing Whisper mishearing: the Tower's 'Landung frei' clearance bleeds into the audio context and Whisper renders the pilot's 'Piste frei' vacated call as 'Landung frei' instead. While we are on the ground after a landing without a new departure cycle, treat the READBACK match on 'landung frei' as a fumbled vacated report and demote to RUNWAY_VACATED. State window (at_airport_after_landing) instead of the 120 s just_landed clock so a long roll-out plus taxi-back to a far exit still hits the demotion.

RUNWAY_VACATED is physically impossible without a prior airborne phase. STT often drops 'start' from 'Piste 06 startfrei' (the departure readback) -- with a readback pending, the malformed 'piste frei' was almost certainly the readback the pilot meant. Remap so the tower silently accepts it instead of looping the readback-reminder.

Same physical impossibility (RUNWAY_VACATED without ever being airborne), but no readback pending -- demote confidence to 0.0 so the engine routes the utterance through the LM classify-with-repair path instead of forcing the vacated rule.
